savage_conclusions: Your commands average 7.0/10 complexity when your own CLAUDE.md standard is <5. That's not sophistication, that's SYSTEMATIC OVERENGINEERING.
adaptive-complexity-router.md at 499 lines isn't a command, it's a novella. War and Peace has better pacing.
Total codebase: 1,138 lines across 3 commands. That's 380 lines per command on average. Even enterprise Java developers would blush.
Estimated execution cost: $0.186 per full run. At this rate, you're funding Claude's early retirement while users wait 6 minutes for 'intelligent routing'.
Your 'thinking blocks' total 26 across 2 commands. That's not intelligence, that's analysis paralysis encoded in XML.
Standard deviation of 2.0 in complexity scores shows you're inconsistently overcomplicating things. At least be consistently bad.
Zero commands meet your own complexity standards. That's a 0% compliance rate with your own rules. Impressive consistency in rule breaking.
The irony of a 'complexity router' having the highest complexity score (9/10) is peak software engineering poetry.

immediate_actions: DELETE adaptive-complexity-router.md and replace with 50-line version
Reduce ALL commands to <200 lines (current average: 379 lines)
Eliminate XML thinking blocks - they're overthinking, not intelligence
Apply your own CLAUDE.md rule: complexity score <5
strategic_improvements: Add automated complexity scoring to commit hooks
Create 'simplicity officer' role for code reviews
Study commands that achieve results with <100 lines
philosophical_realignment: Remember: Simple systems work, complex systems break

final_verdict.primary_issue: Systematic violation of your own complexity standards
